task_completion.description: Navigate to the Duplicate Items view or identify duplicates in the library
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Duplicate Items' collection is selected in the left pane of Zotero, or duplicate pairs are clearly visible and being examined in the main library view.

task_completion.description: Select duplicate copies of a paper to prepare for merging
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple copies of the same paper are highlighted/selected simultaneously in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: Execute the merge action for the selected duplicates
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Merge 2 items' (or similar) button is clicked in the right-hand pane of the Zotero interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the library contains exactly 10 unique papers after merging
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main library view ('My Library') shows exactly 10 top-level items in the middle pane, with no duplicate titles remaining.

task_completion.description: Verify that reading notes were preserved on all surviving items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The remaining 10 items in the library show an expansion arrow or note icon indicating an attached note, or expanding the items reveals the preserved notes.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Zotero graphical user interface to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Zotero application window being actively used, rather than terminal commands modifying the Zotero SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual merge feature rather than just deleting the bare copies
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking the 'Merge' button in the right pane, rather than right-clicking items and selecting 'Move to Trash' or pressing the delete key.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the real Zotero application
integrity.visual_evidence: The Zotero interface appears standard and functional, with correct OS window decorations, rather than a mock-up or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Zotero library initially contains 20 items (10 unique neuroscience papers, each duplicated once). Copy A of each paper has exactly one note attached, while Copy B has no notes. The final expected state is exactly 10 unique papers in the library, with all 10 papers retaining their attached note. Known paper titles include 'Synaptic Plasticity and Memory: An Evaluation of the Hypothesis', 'Grid Cells and the Entorhinal Map of Space', 'A default mode of brain function', and 'Mirror Neurons and the Simulation Theory of Mind-Reading'.
